Chemistry Lesson
Topic: Acid-Base Reactions
Class: SSS1 | Board: NERDC

Objective: Students will design and construct a pH scale from household materials.

Activity (25 min):
Chioma helps distribute the pH indicator strips. Nkechi records results on the board. Obinna mixes solutions carefully.

The class designs a procedure to test household substances: lemon juice, soap, water, baking soda.
Students construct their own pH reference chart. Each group creates a visual scale from 0-14.

Assessment:
Students produce an annotated pH scale diagram and develop a conclusion about which substances are safe to mix.

Closure:
Homework: Design an experiment to test one more household substance and produce a prediction before testing.
reference: NERDC SSS1 Chemistry lesson on acid-base reactions where students design and conduct experiments, construct pH scales, and create a neutralisation reference chart.
subject: chemistry
notes: Rules-only correction: (1) CREATE verbs added (design, construct, create, produce, develop). (2) Objective/Activity/Assessment labels added. (3) Names replaced with Nigerian equivalents (Chioma, Nkechi, Obinna). Template-based corrections improve surface metrics but lesson is still less scaffolded than a full RAQ LLM revision would produce.
